processing_time_ranges: 35 minutes (Majirel / Majirel Cool Cover / Majirel Cool Inforced / Majirouge — printed as 'Development time: 35 minutes')
50 minutes (Majirel High-Lift only — printed as 'Development time: 50 minutes' on 07/23 chart)
gray_coverage_rules: 100% grey coverage claim (2025 chart: 'Same 100% grey coverage'; 2023 chart: '100% grey coverage').
Under 50% white: Choose the client's desired Majirel shade.
Over 50% white, warm target shade: ½ tube of the desired shade + ½ tube of the corresponding level Gold shade (.3/G).
Over 50% white, cool target shade: ½ tube of the desired shade + ½ tube of the corresponding level Natural shade (N) (2023 chart prints '-/N').
2023 chart (P2634200): 6.03, 7.03 and 8.03 give 'Complete coverage without mixing'.
Majirel Cool Cover, Level 3-5: For up to 100% coverage of white hair, apply target shade without mixing.

Majirel Cool Cover, Level 6 & above: For up to 100% natural coverage of white hair, apply target shade; for up to 100% opaque coverage of white hair apply ½ target shade + ½ natural shade on the same level.
Majirel High-Lift (2023 chart): grey blending only — up to 30% white, apply target shade + 30-vol Majicrème Developer; 'Majirel High-Lift is not designed for white coverage.'
lift_rules: Majirel High-Lift (2023 chart, 1:2 ratio): 30-vol up to 3 levels (maximum neutralization); 40-vol up to 4½ levels (maximum lift).
special_usage_notes: Mixing guideline as printed (2025 chart): '1 to 1.5 mixing ratio. 1 tube + 3 oz. Majicrème Developer. Measure 3 oz. of Majicrème Developer 12.5-vol., 20-vol., or 30-vol. depending on the desired intensity of the result.'
Mixing guideline as printed (2023 chart, P2634200): '1 : 1.5 mixing ratio: 1.7 oz. tube + 2.6 oz. Majicrème Developer.'
2023 chart safety panel: 'USE EXCLUSIVELY WITH MAJICRÈME DEVELOPERS, 12.5-VOL (3.75%), 20-VOL. (6%), 30-VOL (9%), 40-VOL (12%) MAXIMUM.'

Carmilane™, Rubilane™ & DM5 WARNING (2025 chart): these shades must be used alone and must not be used in combination with any other shade from a different technology family (e.g. Carmilane™ 5.60 can be mixed with Carmilane™ 6.66). 2023 chart extends the same warning to Lumax (Carmilane™ 5.60/5RRR cannot be mixed with Lumax 6.60/6RRR).

Majirel Boosters (2025 chart): Blue, Violet, Green, Copper, Clear; mixed as '.25 Majirel Booster + Majirel Shade + Developer', development time 35 minutes. 'If the violet booster is used with a level 8 and up, be cautious and use a very small amount.'

Majirel Clear (2023 chart): break the base; subdue the intensity of tone; create a lighter shade; dilute another shade for color-matching/refreshing on mid-lengths and ends; create pastel shades with mixers, Carmilane, Rubilane, Lumax, or DM5 shades.
Mix shades (2023 chart): Cool Mix .10 Blue Mix, .70 Green Mix, .20 Violet Mix; Warm Mix .40 Copper Mix, .60 Red Mix; plus 901S/NB and 900S/NN.

Cool Cover reflect labels as printed on 2023 chart: .11/BB 'Ash+', .1/B 'Ash', .2/V 'Violet', .0/N 'Neutral', .13/BG 'Beige' (Cool Cover section only; the main dual-coding legend on both charts prints .1 as 'blue').
Level legend (both charts): 10 pale blonde, 9 very light blonde, 8 light blonde, 7 blonde, 6 dark blonde, 5 light brown, 4 brown, 3 dark brown, 2 darkest brown, 1 black.
Dual coding example as printed: '5GV = 5.32 — Level of color / Primary tone of color / Secondary tone of color'.
Mocha (.8/M) placement note (2023 chart): '.8 LEVELS 4, 5, & 6' and '.8 LEVELS 7 & 8' printed against the color wheel.
2025 chart 'Improved' formula claims: odor trapping technology for reduced ammonia odor; larger 2 oz. tube; same 100% grey coverage; same 1 to 1.5 mixing ratio and 35-minute processing time.

Rinsing (2025 chart): emulsify with warm water, rinse, shampoo twice with L'Oréal Professionnel Post-Color Shampoo. Rinsing (2023 chart): rinse and shampoo with Metal Detox Shampoo, follow with Metal Detox mask.
Allergy warning printed on 2023 chart: skin allergy test 48 hours before each use; wear gloves; do not use on clients with prior reactions or sensitive/itchy/damaged scalp.
line_technical_records.source_document: Majirel Color Chart 2025 (LPSEPMAJ03 - 09/25); supplemented by Majirel Color Chart (P2634200 - 07/23)
data_gaps: The two charts state the 1:1.5 ratio differently in ounces (2025: 1 tube + 3 oz. developer, reflecting the larger 2 oz. tube; 2023: 1.7 oz. tube + 2.6 oz. developer). Both printed versions are recorded; no reconciliation beyond what is printed.
The main dual-coding legend prints .1 as 'blue' (B), not 'Ash'; 'Ash' wording appears only in the 2023 Cool Cover section labels. No legend entry was invented beyond printed wording.
12.5-vol lift capability (levels of lift) is not stated on either chart; only 20-vol and 30-vol lift levels are printed for core Majirel.
Neither chart states a developer strength explicitly tied to grey coverage for core Majirel (developer choice is printed only as 'depending on the desired intensity of the result').

lift_rules: Zero lift (printed: 'Zero ammonia. Zero lift. Ultra conditioning.').
'Dia Light has an acid pH close to the natural pH of the hair. There is no lift with gentle penetration of colorants for long lasting color.'
special_usage_notes: Preparation as printed: '1 : 1.5 mixing ratio — 1 tube (1.7 oz.) to 2.6 oz. Diactivateur developer. Measure 2.6 oz. of 6-vol. (1.8%) or 9-vol. (2.7%) Diactivateur Developer, depending on the desired intensity of the result.'

Application: on dry hair, spray Metal Detox Pre-Treatment on each section and apply Dia Light formula with a brush or a bottle on regrowth, mid-lengths or ends; on wet hair, after shampooing with Metal Detox Shampoo, apply with a brush or bottle on pre-lightened hair.

Dia Light Liquifier: best for fast bottle application and toning all over; Step 1 mix Dia Light shade + Diactivateur developer at 1:1.5; Step 2 add Liquifier (half dose .2 oz., full dose .4 oz.); 'Liquifier only to be used with Dia Light + Diactivateur developer. Liquifier is not a developer.' Uses: fast application, ultimate customization, adjust color intensity (increase transparency and shine).
Dia DM5 & Rubilane™ WARNING: 'It is imperative that DM5 & Rubilane™ shades be used alone. They must not be used in combination with any other shade.'

Claims printed: '+30% more conditioning* (*vs. uncolored hair)'; conditions hair after lightening and color services; gel-crème consistency for controlled, precision application; cationic polymers have a resurfacing effect on the cuticle.
Mocha composition as printed: 'Mocha .8/M Contains: Violet .2/V, Blue .1/B, Gold .3/G'.
Dual coding example as printed: '5GV = 5.32 — Level of color / Primary tone of color / Secondary tone of color'.
Level legend: 10 pale blonde, 9 very light blonde, 8 light blonde, 7 blonde, 6 dark blonde, 5 light brown, 4 brown, 3 dark brown, 2 darkest brown, 1 black.
Rinsing: when development time is complete, add a little warm water and gently emulsify; rinse thoroughly and shampoo with Metal Detox Shampoo; follow with Metal Detox mask.
2 NEW coral shades flagged on the 2025 chart: 10.24/10VC and 8.24/8VC.
data_gaps: No grey/white coverage or blending instructions are printed on the Dia Light 2025 chart; gray_coverage_rules left empty.
No developer-to-result mapping beyond 'depending on the desired intensity of the result' is printed for 6-vol vs 9-vol.
The chart prints only the document code LPJUNDL01 with no MM/YY revision date.
No processing-time differentiation by hair condition or service type is printed beyond the single flexible 2-20 minute range.

issue_log: Majirel 2025 chart (LPSEPMAJ03) and 2023 chart (P2634200) print the 1:1.5 mixing ratio with different ounce quantities (1 tube + 3 oz. vs 1.7 oz. tube + 2.6 oz.); this reflects the 2025 'larger 2oz tube' change and both are recorded verbatim, with the 2025 chart treated as current.

The reflect-digit legend on these US charts prints .1 as 'blue' (dual code B), not 'Ash'; 'Ash'/'Ash+' wording appears only as Cool Cover section labels on the 2023 chart (.1/B Ash, .11/BB Ash+). Recorded exactly as printed rather than normalizing to 'Ash'.

The 40-vol (12%) Majicrème option and the Majirel High-Lift 1:2 ratio / 50-minute / 40-vol up-to-4½-levels rules appear only on the 2023 chart (P2634200); the 2025 chart (LPSEPMAJ03) does not mention 40-vol or High-Lift. Both recorded with their source documents identified.

pypdf text extraction of the 2023 Majirel chart shows apparent typos in the shade grid (e.g. '5.35/7GRv' and '4.35/7GRv' on the 2025 chart rows for levels 5 and 4); these affect shade-grid cells only, not the line-level technical rules recorded here.
Official product page and shade-charts hub were not fetched: the three PDFs contained all required line-level technical content, so no additional sources were used.
